description: Singapore's PSG pre-approval requires every vendor to meet a baseline of regulatory and cybersecurity requirements regardless of which ERP module they cover. These apply uniformly across all 89 PSG solution categories.

groups.name: Cyber Essentials Mark (CEM)
groups.description: Singapore's CSA Cyber Essentials Mark is a baseline cybersecurity certification. PSG-approved vendors must attain CEM for ICT Vendors (if they are the Product Principal) or hold an equivalent accepted certification (if they are a reseller). This is the cornerstone of PSG's compliance floor.

checklist.text: Do you hold Cyber Essentials Mark certification as the Product Principal?
checklist.rationale: Confirms vendor meets PSG's baseline cybersecurity requirement for solution originators.

source_refs.text: Are you the Product Principal of the solution that you are submitting for pre-approval
🟡 Preferred - CEM for Product Principal

checklist.text: If not Product Principal, do you hold an accepted equivalent certification?
checklist.rationale: Allows resellers to demonstrate compliance via alternative recognized cybersecurity standards.

source_refs.text: Are you the Product Principal of the solution that you are submitting for pre-approval
🟡 Preferred - CEM for Product Principal

checklist.text: Can you provide current CEM certificate or equivalent certification documentation?
checklist.rationale: Enables PSG to verify compliance status before pre-approval is granted.

source_refs.text: 🟡 Preferred - CEM for Product Principal

groups.name: Personal Data Protection (PDPA)
groups.description: Singapore's Personal Data Protection Act (PDPA) governs how digital solutions collect, use, disclose, process, and dispose of personal data. PSG requires every pre-approved solution to support PDPA obligations — consent capture, purpose limitation, retention rules, and access rights.

checklist.text: Solution collects, uses, discloses, processes, or disposes of personal data
checklist.rationale: Determines whether PDPA obligations apply; foundational trigger for all downstream requirements.

source_refs.text: Does your digital solution collect, use, disclose, process or dispose personal data

checklist.text: Vendor completed Personal Data Protection Requirements form at go.gov.sg/pdp
checklist.rationale: PSG mandates formal attestation; demonstrates vendor has assessed and documented PDPA compliance obligations.

source_refs.text: To comply with this requirement, you MUST complete the Personal Data Protection Requirements form at https://go.gov.sg/pdp.

checklist.text: Solution enforces role-based access controls and multi-factor authentication for personal data
checklist.rationale: Ensures only authorised users access personal data; core control for PDPA access rights and security.

source_refs.text: Have strict access controls in place to ensure that only authorised users can access the recorded footage, including measures such as multi-factor authentication and role-based access controls

checklist.text: Solution encrypts personal data at rest and in transit
checklist.rationale: Protects personal data confidentiality; required safeguard under PDPA and PSG cybersecurity baseline.

source_refs.text: Allow encryption of video footage that can only be accessed by authorised users

checklist.text: Solution implements automated data retention and deletion policies per PDPA
checklist.rationale: Enforces purpose limitation and retention rules; prevents unauthorised data hoarding.

source_refs.text: Digital solutions that collect, use, disclose, process or dispose personal data should incorporate features that support the obligations under the Personal Data Protection Act (2020).
Can your solution demonstrate compliance with the following Personal Data Protection requirements

checklist.text: Solution maintains regular data backups with documented recovery procedures
checklist.rationale: Ensures business continuity and data integrity; supports PDPA obligation to protect personal data.

source_refs.text: Conduct regular data backup to ensure data recovery in the event of a system failure or other disaster

groups.name: Vulnerability Assessment & Penetration Testing (VA/PT)
groups.description: PSG requires vendors to submit a comprehensive VA/PT report dated no more than 12 months before checklist submission, conducted by a qualified third party. The scope must cover network security, application security, data protection, access control, API security, and (where applicable) cloud security.

checklist.text: VA/PT report dated within 12 months before checklist submission by qualified third party.
checklist.rationale: Ensures assessment is current and conducted by competent external auditor, not self-assessed.

source_refs.text: Please submit the VA/PT report (dated maximum 1 year from the checklist submission date). The VA/PT Report must include Executive summary; Detailed findings and risk ratings; Remediation recommendations; Evidence of vulnerability fixes or mitigation plans; Testing methodology used; Scope of assessment; Assessor's qualifications and certifications.

Has your solution undergone a comprehensive security vulnerability assessment/penetration testing (VA/PT) conducted by a qualified third-party within the last 12 months? The scope of the VA/PT must cover network security; application security; data protection measures and access control (if applicable); API security testing (if applicable); Cloud security configuration review (if applicable). Specifically, for web application security, the scope must cover minimally all OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities.
Has your solution undergone a comprehensive security vulnerability assessment/penetration testing (VA/PT) conducted by a qualified third-party within the last 12 months

checklist.text: VA/PT scope covers network, application, data protection, access control, and API security.
checklist.rationale: Mandates comprehensive testing across all critical attack surfaces relevant to ERP systems.

source_refs.text: The scope of the VA/PT must cover network security; application security; data protection measures and access control (if applicable); API security testing (if applicable); Cloud security configuration review (if applicable). Specifically, for web application security, the scope must cover minimally all OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities.

Has your solution undergone a comprehensive security vulnerability assessment/penetration testing (VA/PT) conducted by a qualified third-party within the last 12 months? The scope of the VA/PT must cover network security; application security; data protection measures and access control (if applicable); API security testing (if applicable); Cloud security configuration review (if applicable). Specifically, for web application security, the scope must cover minimally all OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities.

checklist.text: VA/PT assessor holds CREST certification or equivalent recognised security professional credential.
checklist.rationale: Guarantees assessor has independently verified expertise to conduct rigorous testing.

source_refs.text: Qualified third-party refers to: CREST-certified companies [ https://www.crest-approved.org/members/] or companies with security professional with relevant CREST certifications; Security professionals with recognised certifications such as: Offensive Security Certified Professional (OSCP); EC-Council Certified Penetration Testing Professional (CPENT); GIAC Penetration Tester (GPEN); or other equivalent industry-recognised certifications.

Qualified third-party refers to: CREST-certified companies [https://www.crest-approved.org/members/] or companies with security professional with relevant CREST certifications; Security professionals with recognised certifications such as: Offensive Security Certified Professional (OSCP); EC-Council Certified Penetration Testing Professional (CPENT); GIAC Penetration Tester (GPEN); or other equivalent industry-recognised certifications.

CREST-certified companies [https://www.crest-approved.org/members/] or companies with security professional with relevant CREST certifications; Security professionals with recognised certifications such as: Offensive Security Certified Professional (OSCP); EC-Council Certified Penetration Testing Professional (CPENT); GIAC Penetration Tester (GPEN); or other equivalent industry-recognised certifications.

checklist.text: VA/PT report includes executive summary, detailed findings with risk ratings, and remediation recommendations.
checklist.rationale: Ensures report is complete, actionable, and demonstrates understanding of vulnerabilities found.

source_refs.text: Please submit the VA/PT report (dated maximum 1 year from the checklist submission date). The VA/PT Report must include Executive summary; Detailed findings and risk ratings; Remediation recommendations; Evidence of vulnerability fixes or mitigation plans; Testing methodology used; Scope of assessment; Assessor's qualifications and certifications.

checklist.text: Cloud security configuration review included in VA/PT scope if solution uses cloud infrastructure.
checklist.rationale: Addresses cloud-specific risks that traditional network testing may not fully cover.

source_refs.text: The scope of the VA/PT must cover network security; application security; data protection measures and access control (if applicable); API security testing (if applicable); Cloud security configuration review (if applicable). Specifically, for web application security, the scope must cover minimally all OWASP Top 10 vulnerabilities.

checklist.text: Resellers provide VA/PT report from product principal or SOC 2 Type II with technical details.
checklist.rationale: Allows resellers to leverage vendor assessments while maintaining evidence of security testing.

source_refs.text: If you are the reseller of the solution, please obtain the VA/PT report from your product principal. SOC 2 Type II report can be accepted if the detailed technical vulnerability assessment results are part of the SOC2 Type II scope.
